ticket.summary: Laptop loses audio whenever VPN reconnects mid-call. Happens in both Teams and Zoom.
ticket.raw_dump:
Ticket Number: T20260218.0100
Title: Teams call drops after VPN reconnect

Contact Info
Name: Jordan Lee
Email: jordan.lee@example.com
Phone: 555-010-5555

Description
Laptop loses audio whenever VPN reconnects mid-call. Happens in both Teams and Zoom,
usually after 30 minutes on hotel Wi-Fi. User rebooted twice and reinstalled the VPN
client last week. Need confirmation that the NIC drivers are current.

Troubleshooting Notes
- Gathered screenshots from Teams notifications.
- Verified ticket generated from mobile email.
- Pending output from systeminfo.

Quoted Thread (should be ignored by sanitizer)
-----Original Message-----
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@example.com>
Sent: Monday, February 17, 2026 8:02 PM
To: MSP Helpdesk <helpdesk@examplemsp.com>
Subject: RE: VPN keeps disconnecting

> We already pushed the update. Does this still happen after reboot?
>
> Thanks,
> Tier 1

Still broken. Please advise.

> On Mon, Feb 17, 2026 at 7:38 PM MSP Helpdesk <helpdesk@examplemsp.com> wrote:
> -----Original Message-----
> From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@example.com>
> Sent: Monday, February 17, 2026 7:15 PM
> Subject: VPN keeps disconnecting
>
> Drops network every 30 minutes. Need this fixed before tomorrow's webinar.

symptoms: Audio drops when VPN reconnects mid-call
Occurs after ~30 minutes on hotel Wi-Fi

impact.how_bad: Cannot participate in calls during VPN reconnects

observations: Audio drops when VPN reconnects mid-call
Occurs after ~30 minutes on hotel Wi-Fi

active_hypotheses.hypothesis: VPN reconnect triggers audio device re-enumeration, dropping active streams
active_hypotheses.discriminating_question: Does audio recover automatically or require manual intervention?
discriminating_tests: Check audio device list before/after VPN reconnect (get_audio_devices)
Review Windows event log for audio service restarts (system_errors)
active_hypotheses.notes: Most common cause — Windows treats VPN adapter changes as hardware events, resetting audio graph.

active_hypotheses.hypothesis: MTU mismatch or QoS policy change on VPN reconnect drops media streams
active_hypotheses.discriminating_question: Do pings to Teams/Zoom servers show packet loss after reconnect?

active_hypotheses.hypothesis: Outdated NIC driver causes instability under VPN load
active_hypotheses.discriminating_question: Is the NIC driver version current per vendor advisory?
discriminating_tests: Look for NIC errors in device manager event log (system_errors)
active_hypotheses.notes: User already mentioned wanting NIC driver confirmation — worth ruling in/out quickly.
